description_long: This utility pole is a tall, vertical wooden structure with a weathered brown finish, typical of those found along urban streets and rural roads. It features two horizontal crossarms, each supporting a set of four ceramic or glass insulators, which are mounted symmetrically on either side of the pole. Four taut utility wires are attached to the insulators, extending outward in both directions, indicating its function in electrical or telecommunication line distribution. The pole is cylindrical and tapers slightly toward the top, with the base appearing robust and solidly anchored. No transformers, streetlights, or identification tags are visible on the pole. The overall design is utilitarian, focusing on structural stability and efficient support for overhead wires.
description: A tall, brown wooden utility pole with two horizontal crossarms and four attached wires, supporting insulators for electrical or telecommunication lines. The pole is sturdy and functional, with no additional fixtures or tags.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the pole, both crossarms, all four insulators, and the attached wires extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the pole and crossarms, with wires visible as thin lines extending from the insulators.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying both crossarms, insulators, and wires, with the pole's cylindrical shape clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the pole's slim profile and the alignment of the crossarms and wires.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Two horizontal crossarms are stacked vertically near the top of the pole, each supporting insulators symmetrically on both sides.
